title: Celebrity Booze: The Ultimate Cash Grab

jokes.text: Any idiot could take a bottle of booze, slap a celebrity's face on it and make a fortune. I mean, we could do it if we wanted.
jokes.setup: Dennis criticizing celebrity booze brands as a sellout cash grab
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggesting they should do the exact same thing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Dennis's complete lack of self-awareness and principles - criticizes something then immediately wants to do it

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Any idiot could take a bottle of booze, slap a celebrity's face on it and make a fortune. I mean, we could do it if we wanted.

jokes.text: Premium. I was gonna say that, dude. Premium. I was gonna say premium.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks what word describes them as businessmen
jokes.punchline: All three immediately say 'premium' with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Shows their delusional self-image - they genuinely believe they're premium people despite all evidence to the contrary

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Premium.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I was gonna say that, dude.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Premium.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I was gonna say premium.

jokes.text: Goldschläger. Goldschläger. Yeah. Goldschläger.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks what the most premium alcohol is
jokes.punchline: All three immediately agree it's Goldschläger - a tacky novelty liqueur
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of their terrible taste - Goldschläger is the opposite of premium, it's a gimmicky party drink

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Goldschläger.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Goldschläger.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah. Goldschläger.

jokes.text: I can get a bunch of nickels and I could smash them up and put them into a 'schläger. We call it Nickelschläger, and we pitch it as the working man's spirit.
jokes.setup: Charlie wants to create their own premium alcohol
jokes.punchline: Suggests putting smashed nickels in schnapps as a 'working man's' alternative
jokes.explanation: Pure Charlie logic - dangerous, nonsensical, but presented with complete confidence. Sets up running gag.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I can get a bunch of nickels and I could smash them up and put them into a 'schläger. We call it Nickelschläger, and we pitch it as the working man's spirit.

jokes.text: Well, that sounds disgusting, and, uh, potentially dangerous as well. Yeah, I'm-a try it, though.
jokes.setup: Dennis correctly identifies Charlie's idea as dangerous
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately says he's going to try it anyway
jokes.explanation: Charlie's total disregard for his own safety in pursuit of a terrible idea

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, that sounds disgusting, and, uh, potentially dangerous as well.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm-a try it, though.

jokes.text: Jägermeister. Yes. Jägermeister. Yeah, yeah. Jägermeister. Jägermeister, yeah.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks what the second classiest drink is
jokes.punchline: Unanimous agreement on Jägermeister - another party drink masquerading as classy
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern of their awful taste - Jäger is college party fuel, not classy

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Jägermeister.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Jägermeister.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Jägermeister.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Jägermeister, yeah.

jokes.text: Those drinks that make people feel premium, you know? So I say we do a blend of all the most premium drinks. We put them all together, and we make one drink called Paddy's SchlägerSchnappsterMeister.
jokes.setup: Dennis wants to blend premium drinks
jokes.punchline: Creates an absurdly long German-sounding portmanteau name
jokes.explanation: Ridiculous name that sounds like a parody of German alcohol brands - unwieldy but memorable

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Those drinks that make people feel premium, you know? So I say we do a blend of all the most premium drinks. We put them all together, and we make one drink called Paddy's SchlägerSchnappsterMeister.

jokes.text: Frank's ringtone: 'I want money, lots and lots'
jokes.setup: Frank's phone ringing at a key business moment
jokes.punchline: His ringtone being a literal song about wanting money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank character detail - completely on the nose about his motivation.

dialogue.line: I want money, lots and lots

jokes.text: I'm in the sky. In the sky? What? That's right, dicknips.
jokes.setup: Gang asks Frank where he is
jokes.punchline: Frank reveals he's on a private jet, calls them 'dicknips'
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual vulgarity and wealth flexing - 'dicknips' is such a Frank insult

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm in the sky.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: In the sky?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That's right, dicknips.

jokes.text: I once had Greg Louganis as the face of my apricot brand in the '80s. But when he cracked his noggin at the Olympics, the business took a nosedive with him.
jokes.setup: Frank explains why he's hesitant about celebrity endorsements
jokes.punchline: 'Nosedive' pun referring to Louganis's diving accident
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about a real Olympic incident with a perfectly executed pun - very Frank

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I once had Greg Louganis as the face of my apricot brand in the '80s. But when he cracked his noggin at the Olympics, the business took a nosedive with him.

jokes.text: Have you ever hear of a little show called Malcolm in the Middle? Have I? Changed the game. Changed the game, Frank.
jokes.setup: Dennis building up their celebrity connection
jokes.punchline: Treats Malcolm in the Middle like it's the most important show ever
jokes.explanation: Their tendency to oversell everything - treating a decent sitcom like it's revolutionary

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Have you ever hear of a little show called Malcolm in the Middle?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Have I? Changed the game. Changed the game, Frank.

jokes.text: And Malcolm. He's here, too, although he's all grown up now, so you might not recognize him.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to name-drop celebrities
jokes.punchline: Refers to Aaron Paul as 'Malcolm' - completely wrong show/character
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis doesn't actually know who these celebrities are - confusing Breaking Bad with Malcolm in the Middle

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: And Malcolm. He's here, too, although he's all grown up now, so you might not recognize him.

jokes.text: How are they gonna bring them to Aruba? We were never going to Aruba. We're circling Philly.
jokes.setup: Dee points out the logical flaw in the plan
jokes.punchline: Frank reveals they're just flying in circles for tax purposes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank logic - elaborate tax fraud scheme that makes no practical sense

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: How are they gonna bring them to Aruba?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: We were never going to Aruba. We're circling Philly.

jokes.text: I got to put enough miles on the jet to make it look like I went to Aruba and back. It's a tax thing.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they're circling Philadelphia
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of tax fraud as if it's perfectly normal
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual criminality - treats fraud like a minor inconvenience

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I got to put enough miles on the jet to make it look like I went to Aruba and back. It's a tax thing.

jokes.text: Charlie dressed as a limo driver with a cap he wants to 'tip' to celebrities
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to dress nicely for the celebrity meeting
jokes.punchline: Looks exactly like a chauffeur and thinks this is appropriate
jokes.explanation: Charlie's complete misunderstanding of appropriate attire - thinks servant clothes are fancy

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Charlie dressed as a limo driver with a cap he wants to 'tip' to celebrities

jokes.text: It's Nickelschläger, man. It's delicious. I've been drinking it all day.
jokes.setup: Charlie offering his homemade alcohol to the others
jokes.punchline: Has actually been consuming the dangerous nickel-infused liquor
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Charlie - follows through on dangerous idea despite warnings

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It's Nickelschläger, man. It's delicious. I've been drinking it all day.

jokes.text: Guys, why is it going off on my stomach? Because you have a belly full of nickels.
jokes.setup: Metal detector going off on Charlie's stomach
jokes.punchline: He's literally full of the nickels he's been drinking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to the setup - the absurd becomes literal and disgusting

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Guys, why is it going off on my stomach?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Because you have a belly full of nickels.

jokes.text: I'll make myself throw up and then I'll get... Should I throw up here or, uh...
jokes.setup: Charlie needs to get the nickels out of his system
jokes.punchline: Casually considers vomiting in public as a solution
jokes.explanation: Charlie's complete lack of social awareness - public vomiting seems reasonable to him

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'll make myself throw up and then I'll get... Should I throw up here or, uh...

jokes.text: I'm going for a 'Jordan in the '90s' look. I mean, who's more premium than MJ, right?
jokes.setup: Mac defending his outfit choice
jokes.punchline: Wearing an ill-fitting suit thinking it makes him look like Michael Jordan
jokes.explanation: Mac's delusions about his appearance and style - thinks he can emulate MJ with a baggy suit

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm going for a 'Jordan in the '90s' look. I mean, who's more premium than MJ, right?

jokes.text: I'm thinking that I go pitch a fashion line and sort of expand into a lifestyle brand.
jokes.setup: Mac getting carried away with his Michael Jordan look
jokes.punchline: Already planning to expand beyond alcohol into fashion
jokes.explanation: Mac's tendency to immediately get ahead of himself and overcomplicate plans

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm thinking that I go pitch a fashion line and sort of expand into a lifestyle brand.

jokes.text: Bathroom on the plane is busted. You can use the emergency can. You're sitting on it.
jokes.setup: Dee needing to use the bathroom on the plane
jokes.punchline: Frank revealing she's been sitting on the emergency toilet the whole time
jokes.explanation: Good cringe setup with Frank's casual attitude toward Dee's discomfort and dignity.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Bathroom on the plane is busted. You can use the emergency can. You're sitting on it.

jokes.text: We don't shake hands. Sorry. We're not shaking hands. We don't know these people.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to start with a firm handshake as planned
jokes.punchline: Celebrities refuse to shake hands, derailing Dennis's entire strategy
jokes.explanation: Post-COVID anxiety played for laughs - ruins Dennis's carefully planned approach

dialogue.line: We don't shake hands. Sorry. We're not shaking hands. We don't know these people.

jokes.text: I think... We can't kick this off without a firm handshake. I really don't mind shaking his hand. How many times do we have to go through this?
jokes.setup: Dennis insisting on handshakes despite celebrity refusal
jokes.punchline: Creates awkward dynamic where Bryan wants to shake but Aaron forbids it
jokes.explanation: Awkward power dynamic between the celebrities mirroring Dennis's frustration

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I think... We can't kick this off without a firm handshake.

dialogue.speaker: Bryan Cranston
dialogue.line: I really don't mind shaking his hand.

dialogue.speaker: Aaron Paul
dialogue.line: How many times do we have to go through this?

jokes.text: Wow, look at that suit. I love that. I'm not supposed to talk.
jokes.setup: Bryan Cranston commenting on Mac's terrible suit
jokes.punchline: Mac being excited about the compliment while admitting he's not supposed to talk
jokes.explanation: Good moment showing Mac's inability to follow instructions when his ego is stroked.

dialogue.speaker: Bryan Cranston
dialogue.line: Wow, look at that suit. I love that.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm not supposed to talk.

jokes.text: It was... Malcolm. He was throwing me a weird vibe. Yeah. He's very aggressive. He's super aggressive.
jokes.setup: Dennis making excuses for the failed pitch
jokes.punchline: Still calling Aaron Paul 'Malcolm' and blaming him for being aggressive
jokes.explanation: Doubling down on the wrong name while making Aaron Paul the villain

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It was... Malcolm. He was throwing me a weird vibe. Yeah. He's very aggressive. He's super aggressive.

jokes.text: Malcolm's not in the middle. He's on top.
jokes.setup: Continuing to treat Aaron Paul as Malcolm
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'Malcolm in the Middle' title to describe his dominance
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on the show title while maintaining the wrong identity

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Malcolm's not in the middle. He's on top.

jokes.text: I've been throwing up for a little bit. I actually kind of can't stop. I think I'm having a reaction to the metals.
jokes.setup: Charlie approaching a stranger for help
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions he can't stop vomiting due to metal poisoning
jokes.explanation: Charlie's matter-of-fact delivery of his self-inflicted medical crisis

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I've been throwing up for a little bit. I actually kind of can't stop. I think I'm having a reaction to the metals.

jokes.text: On my next hurl, you know, do you... Could you see if you see any loose change in there?
jokes.setup: Charlie asking a stranger for help with his vomiting
jokes.punchline: Wants the stranger to inspect his vomit for nickels
jokes.explanation: Peak Charlie - asking a stranger to examine his vomit for coins is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: On my next hurl, you know, do you... Could you see if you see any loose change in there?

jokes.text: Charlie vomiting on someone's car and then volunteering to watch it
jokes.setup: Charlie continuing to vomit uncontrollably
jokes.punchline: Vomits on a car then offers to guard it from other vandals
jokes.explanation: Charlie's helpful nature even while being destructive - offers to watch the car he just damaged

dialogue.line: Charlie vomiting on someone's car and then volunteering to watch it

jokes.text: Hey, man, are you the Dos Hombres driver? Who? You know, the Malcolm in the Middle guys. Yes, I am.
jokes.setup: Stranger looking for celebrity driver
jokes.punchline: Charlie lies and claims to be their driver despite being a vomiting mess
jokes.explanation: Charlie's opportunistic lying - sees a chance and takes it despite being completely unqualified

dialogue.speaker: Stranger
dialogue.line: Hey, man, are you the Dos Hombres driver? Who? You know, the Malcolm in the Middle guys.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yes, I am.

jokes.text: You always were good against the rush. Now, that is true. You are good against the ru... Now, you're not gonna get away from us, though. We gotcha.
jokes.setup: Cornering Jalen Hurts in a bathroom
jokes.punchline: Making football puns while essentially trapping him
jokes.explanation: Awkward sports references while being threatening - classic cringe territory

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You always were good against the rush.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Now, that is true. You are good against the ru...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Now, you're not gonna get away from us, though. We gotcha.

jokes.text: Imagine this lifestyle: you're on a Caribbean island in an ill-fitting suit.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to pitch Jalen Hurts
jokes.punchline: Describes uncomfortable formal wear on a tropical vacation as aspirational
jokes.explanation: Mac's complete misunderstanding of luxury - thinks sweating in a suit is premium

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Imagine this lifestyle: you're on a Caribbean island in an ill-fitting suit.

jokes.text: Gritty? Wait... What the hell is this? I thought you said Jalen Hurts came in here.
jokes.setup: Looking for Jalen Hurts in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Find Gritty, the Philadelphia Flyers mascot, instead
jokes.explanation: Great visual misdirect - expecting a football player, finding a bizarre mascot

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Gritty? Wait... What the hell is this? I thought you said Jalen Hurts came in here.

jokes.text: How do you confuse a young Black man with a giant orange monster?
jokes.setup: Mac's mistaken identity between Jalen Hurts and Gritty
jokes.punchline: Points out how impossible this confusion should be
jokes.explanation: Dennis's rare moment of clarity pointing out Mac's obvious mistake

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: How do you confuse a young Black man with a giant orange monster?

jokes.text: Okay, Gritty, picture yourself on a Caribbean island.
jokes.setup: Dennis telling Mac not to pitch Gritty
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately starts pitching Gritty anyway
jokes.explanation: Mac's inability to stop pitching even to a mascot - pure determination without reason

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Okay, Gritty, picture yourself on a Caribbean island.

jokes.text: I did vomit in here earlier. Oh, oh, great. Thank you for that. Wow.
jokes.setup: Celebrities noticing a bad smell in Charlie's car
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually admits to vomiting in the vehicle he's driving them in
jokes.explanation: Charlie's complete lack of shame about contaminating their ride

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I did vomit in here earlier.

dialogue.speaker: Aaron Paul
dialogue.line: Oh, oh, great. Thank you for that. Wow.

jokes.text: We can go by whatever code name you feel most comfortable with, sir.
jokes.setup: Aaron Paul correcting Charlie about his name not being Malcolm
jokes.punchline: Charlie thinking 'Aaron' is a code name for security reasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic, showing his conspiracy-minded thinking about celebrity security protocols.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: We can go by whatever code name you feel most comfortable with, sir.

jokes.text: I haven't driven a car in a long, long time.
jokes.setup: Charlie apologizing for his erratic driving
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting he doesn't really know how to drive while driving celebrities
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Charlie's dangerous incompetence and casual attitude about it.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I haven't driven a car in a long, long time.

jokes.text: My name is not Mr. Malcolm, it's Aaron. Okay, I got you. Yeah, yeah. We can go by whatever code name you feel most comfortable with, sir.
jokes.setup: Aaron Paul correcting Charlie about his name
jokes.punchline: Charlie thinks 'Aaron' is a code name and 'Malcolm' is still his real identity
jokes.explanation: Charlie's conspiracy mindset - can't accept he was wrong, so Aaron must be lying

dialogue.speaker: Aaron Paul
dialogue.line: My name is not Mr. Malcolm, it's Aaron.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Okay, I got you. Yeah, yeah. We can go by whatever code name you feel most comfortable with, sir.

jokes.text: When I first stepped onto the boards in the Great White Way of Broadway... Oh, and here we go again about Broadway.
jokes.setup: Bryan starting to tell a Broadway story
jokes.punchline: Aaron's exasperated reaction shows this is a constant occurrence
jokes.explanation: Dynamic between the celebrities - Bryan as self-aggrandizing bore, Aaron as fed-up partner

dialogue.speaker: Bryan Cranston
dialogue.line: When I first stepped onto the boards in the Great White Way of Broadway...

dialogue.speaker: Aaron Paul
dialogue.line: Oh, and here we go again about Broadway.

jokes.text: This clown lost all of his money playing slots at Pechanga. Yeah. It... I-I... No, you have a problem.
jokes.setup: Bryan talking about earning success
jokes.punchline: Aaron reveals Bryan's gambling addiction and financial ruin
jokes.explanation: Sharp reversal - the 'successful' actor is actually broke and addicted

dialogue.speaker: Aaron Paul
dialogue.line: This clown lost all of his money playing slots at Pechanga.

dialogue.speaker: Bryan Cranston
dialogue.line: Yeah. It... I-I...

dialogue.speaker: Aaron Paul
dialogue.line: No, you have a problem.

jokes.text: He was a week away from sucking off tourists, okay? To pay his rent, and you know that.
jokes.setup: Aaron describing Bryan's financial desperation
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of how low Bryan would have sunk
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation of Bryan's rock bottom - goes from gambling to prostitution

dialogue.line: He was a week away from sucking off tourists, okay? To pay his rent, and you know that.

jokes.text: That can is from a shellfish company I started with Tony Danza back in the '80s. Tony's Oysters in a Can-za?
jokes.setup: Dee ate old oysters from a random can
jokes.punchline: Frank reveals it's from his failed business with Tony Danza, including pun name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank backstory - ridiculous celebrity partnership with terrible pun name

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That can is from a shellfish company I started with Tony Danza back in the '80s. Tony's Oysters in a Can-za?

jokes.text: Why don't you just shut your eyes and go to sleep? And then I will let you know when you can wake up. Okay? Shh. Okay. Oh, but sleep's for pussies.
jokes.setup: Aaron trying to get Bryan to stop talking
jokes.punchline: Bryan agrees to sleep then immediately contradicts himself with macho posturing
jokes.explanation: Bryan's inability to stay quiet even when he agrees to - self-defeating behavior

dialogue.speaker: Aaron Paul
dialogue.line: Why don't you just shut your eyes and go to sleep? And then I will let you know when you can wake up. Okay? Shh.

dialogue.speaker: Bryan Cranston
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: Bryan Cranston
dialogue.line: Oh, but sleep's for pussies.

jokes.text: This plane, this pretty little bird can be yours for a mere price of $35 mil.
jokes.setup: Frank introducing himself to the celebrities
jokes.punchline: Immediately tries to sell them his plane instead of the alcohol pitch
jokes.explanation: Frank's opportunistic nature - can't help but try to make a different sale

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: This plane, this pretty little bird can be yours for a mere price of $35 mil.

jokes.text: We're here to sell you a, uh... Nickelschläger! No, goddamn it! None of that.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to get the pitch back on track
jokes.punchline: Charlie blurts out his dangerous alcohol creation
jokes.explanation: Charlie's inability to read the room - still promoting his toxic creation

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We're here to sell you a, uh... Nickelschläger! No, goddamn it! None of that.

jokes.text: We're gonna start all over, and we're gonna do it with a firm handshake. Oh. I'm not supposed to.
jokes.setup: Dennis still obsessed with getting a handshake
jokes.punchline: Bryan wants to shake but is still forbidden by Aaron
jokes.explanation: Dennis's fixation coming full circle - still can't get past the first step

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We're gonna start all over, and we're gonna do it with a firm handshake.

dialogue.speaker: Bryan Cranston
dialogue.line: Oh. I'm not supposed to.

jokes.text: I think you're in an abusive relationship. Put your hand down, Bryan. Put it down, you silly little bitch.
jokes.setup: Mac observing the celebrity dynamic
jokes.punchline: Aaron's controlling behavior escalates to verbal abuse
jokes.explanation: Dark turn showing actual toxic relationship dynamics between the celebrities

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I think you're in an abusive relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Aaron Paul
dialogue.line: Put your hand down, Bryan. Put it down, you silly little bitch.

jokes.text: Bryan's extended dramatic monologue about being controlled and deceived
jokes.setup: Tension building between Bryan and Aaron
jokes.punchline: Bryan delivers an intense dramatic performance about their power dynamic
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly unhinged performance that builds genuine tension before the reveal

jokes.text: Boom. And that, my friends, is acting. What? Wow! That was... That-that was all fake?
jokes.setup: Intense dramatic confrontation that seemed real
jokes.punchline: Bryan reveals it was all an acting exercise
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - builds genuine tension then deflates it brilliantly. Meta-commentary on acting.

dialogue.speaker: Bryan Cranston
dialogue.line: Boom. And that, my friends, is acting.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Wow! That was... That-that was all fake?

jokes.text: You should consider doing a drama, Mr. Middle. You really should consider doing drama.
jokes.setup: Dennis impressed by Bryan's acting performance
jokes.punchline: Still calls him 'Mr. Middle' and suggests he try drama (he's already an acclaimed dramatic actor)
jokes.explanation: Dennis's continued ignorance - suggests Bryan try drama to Bryan Cranston of Breaking Bad

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You should consider doing a drama, Mr. Middle. You really should consider doing drama.

jokes.text: Hey, so listen, do you guys want to invest? Boss? Fuck no. Fuck no! No. Fuck no!
jokes.setup: Dennis finally getting to make his pitch after everything
jokes.punchline: Immediate, emphatic, repeated rejection from both celebrities
jokes.explanation: Perfect climax - after all the buildup and chaos, instant categorical rejection

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Hey, so listen, do you guys want to invest?

dialogue.speaker: Bryan Cranston
dialogue.line: Boss?

dialogue.speaker: Aaron Paul
dialogue.line: Fuck no.

dialogue.speaker: Bryan Cranston
dialogue.line: Fuck no!

dialogue.speaker: Aaron Paul
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Bryan Cranston
dialogue.line: Fuck no!

jokes.text: You people are insane. Get me off this plane.
jokes.setup: Everything that's happened on the plane
jokes.punchline: Aaron's final assessment and desperate escape attempt
jokes.explanation: Perfect summation of the normal person's reaction to the gang's insanity

dialogue.speaker: Aaron Paul
dialogue.line: You people are insane. Get me off this plane.
